programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: The epic adventure reality contest continues. With five Predators and two Prey, the players face a brutal reset - and a shock decision delivers the biggest upset of the game so far.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: We look back at the Clayton-Fisher family, who wanted to transform their dark and dingy attic. Struggling for space in their two-bed terrace, Sarah thought it was time to move on, but Pepper dug her heels in and wanted to convert the loft.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George helps a couple whose Weymouth home was destroyed overnight by a flash flood, and revisits a couple in Chiswick to find out whats next on their demolition list.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Its summer and time for the sheep to be sheared. Matt builds a free-range grazing area for the chickens. And will the farm have its first ever honey harvest?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Spain, Jo and Ken try to turn a profit with their musical side hustle. Colin faces a difficult decision in Bulgaria. In France, will Jon and Angelas wedding be a wash out?

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine gets stuck in with the renovation. All seems to be going well... until a medical emergency puts everything on hold, and Jasmines mum Vasoulla comes to the rescue!

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Cankiz longs to fulfil her dream of living near Torrevieja in Spain, but her daughter wants to stay near her friends in Northamptonshire. Can they agree on where their new home should be?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary and Alison are looking for a rural holiday home in the Haute-Vienne area of central France. Can Laura Hamilton find them the perfect place for their budget of £100k?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: New Zealander Steve Levy is first to host in Exeter and has gone to town with his home inspired menu, cooking prawns for his starter, fish for his main and not one, but two desserts.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music-mad foodie Richard Kaye is second to host in Exeter and is confident he can impress his guests with his culinary know-how. He especially hopes to dazzle one guest in particular: Estelle Macrow.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-assured PR bod Glen King is confident she will be the `hostess with the mostest in Exeter, and has gone all out to impress, even co-ordinating her dishes with her surname.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Exeter, Sarah Wallis has been holding back, allowing her fellow diners to have their time in the sun. Tonight she is ready to explode out of her shell with a silly but hilarious evening including some rather unusual cocktails.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night in Exeter and singleton Estelle Macrow is keen to discover whether she has it in her to successfully host a dinner party.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on their best budget-stretching renovations, from a cramped three-bed semi in Long Eaton to a not-so-done-up terrace in Shropshire.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: In Stratford-upon-Avon, George Clarke helps Laura and Jonnie to transform their mock-Tudor Edwardian house. And in Essex, Nickis 1920s cottage needs major changes.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Scooby the Great Dane seeks a new recruit for the gang. Its a double date for the Davies family with a Labrador and German Shepherd. And is Fox the Pomeranian about to get lucky?

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: In Nottingham, Emma and Matts hectic schedules mixed with two energetic kids is a recipe for mealtime mayhem. Can they turn chaotic convenience into bountiful batching?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aleshe make decorated traybakes, tarte tatin and recreate their childhood toys in biscuits.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Naturist-loving Jess and Ben explore a non-monogamous lifestyle in the hot tub with Olivia and Gage, where Jess faces a big fear. And Kirsty and Tom consider attending a huge orgy.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie are missing one thing from their family: a woman to turn them into a throuple. And glamour puss Chrissie is looking for husband number four.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew battle to save Gerrys partially amputated hand in Stowmarket.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Adrian has injuries to his face and has been bitten on his arm breaking up a fight in the city centre. Andys had a motorbike accident. And Connors fallen 12 feet off a roof.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: There is a hair-raising pursuit through the market town of Ripley which sees a driver mounting pavements, hitting cars and even colliding with an ambulance. An Interceptor has a narrow escape when an out of control driver crashes into his car. A drunk driver goes for the world record in failed breathalyser tests and the helicopter is called in when a Land Rover driver decides to take a pursuit off-road.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Eurocopter gets down and dirty in a lengthy manhunt, a vanload of villains lose control in a high speed pursuit and a drunk driver crushes his Corsa like it is a tin can. Elsewhere, a speed freak thinks he can outrun the law, there is aggravation and abuse on Derbyshires mean streets and the interceptors facing off against an eight legged enemy.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.desc: A biker with a history of stealing vehicles doesnt know how to drive his motorcycle; a man in- between jobs takes up a sketchy method of earning money; Deputies strongly question the legitimacy of a mans work van.

programme.desc: Jared and Johnnys epic mission grinds to a halt, Roscoe battles the elements on a world famous rally, and Turbo is flagged down by the boys in blue.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: When a fully loaded HGV rolls over on the A6, Fluff and his team need to create a second mess to solve the first. Jim is sent to recover a broken down truck from a waste tip facility, but working among the rubbish is not particularly pleasant.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past, Jon Peacock and police dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars, Sarah Capes gets a whiff of something illegal, PC Templeman pulls over a faulty white van and Dan Brigginshaw uncovers a drivers stash.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree. Barry deals with a case of some very hot dogs locked inside a car.

programme.desc: Kev and Pete pursue a runaway car into a pitch-black farmers field, Jacko takes part in a major heroin bust, and rookie police dog Elsa chases a runaway burglar through the streets of Middlesbrough.

programme.title: Cornwalls Red River
programme.desc: Poet John Wedgwood Clarke explores the Red River in Cornwall, a watercourse barely more than a stream that has been heavily polluted by centuries of tin mining. Now, this powerful symbol of Cornish identity, home to some very rare, beautiful and resilient species, is slowly showing signs of coming back to life. Extracts from Johns poem, `Red River at the A30 Culvert, reveal his mixed thoughts about the rivers toxic legacy.

programme.title: All About Eve
programme.desc: Six Oscars went to this account of an ambitious actress who manipulates and connives her way to stardom. Margo employs Eve Harrington to shore up her fragile ego, but the starlet has other plans in mind.

programme.title: Wogan
programme.desc: Classic interview from 1987 in which Terry Wogan talks to Hollywood legend Bette Davis.

programme.title: The Ipcress File
programme.desc: Agent Harry Palmer is assigned to investigate a bizarre brain drain among scientists, and finds himself embroiled in a world of espionage where nobody can be trusted and nothing is what it seems.

programme.title: The Truth about Len Deighton
programme.desc: Profile of best-selling author Len Deighton, whose output ranges from the Harry Palmer espionage thrillers to books on military history and cookery. Contributors include Michael Caine and Max Hastings. First shown in 2006.

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: The birdsong of sunrise in all its uninterrupted glory, free from the voice over and music of traditional television.

programme.title: Fred Dibnahs Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: Focuses on structures that have been built for arts, leisure and entertainment purposes, including the Globe Theatre, the Victoria and Albert Museum and Blackpool Tower.

programme.title: Cornwalls Red River
programme.desc: Poet John Wedgwood Clarke explores the Red River in Cornwall, a watercourse barely more than a stream that has been heavily polluted by centuries of tin mining. Now, this powerful symbol of Cornish identity, home to some very rare, beautiful and resilient species, is slowly showing signs of coming back to life. Extracts from Johns poem, `Red River at the A30 Culvert, reveal his mixed thoughts about the rivers toxic legacy.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: A gang illegally digging up roads to divert electricity to power dozens of cannabis farms was caught, plus a shoddy builder takes £35000 from an unwitting victim.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Presenters cast their expert eyes over a number of properties before they go to auction, giving their opinion on the respective buildings and their potential.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and the teams shop for antiques in Harrogate with experts Izzie Balmer and John Cameron before heading to auction in Beverley. Danny visits an exhibition about pioneering pilot Amy Johnson.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Brighton, Zoe Ball wants to help retired woodworker Neville get his health back on track after a series of heart attacks. Her prescription is delightfully simple: add a little sunshine into his life.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Near Wigan, Matt has built his wifes dream home, and he has filled it, not only with his own collections, but also those of his late father. Its a dire situation for his wife of 25 years, Paula, who is desperate to clear her husbands clutter.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Kents charming cottages and picturesque countryside are calling to a mum and son, ready to settle there after years living overseas. With their budget of £350,000, Denise Nurse is on a mission to find them a place to call home.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: In Ayrshire, a window signed by the poet Robert Burns and a life-changing historic Scottish canoe are both lovingly repaired by the team.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Shauna, Andy, Doniya, Kathryn and Martin race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Featuring most talked-about stories in the UK and interactions with guests on the green sofa.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Residents gather for the opening of Knight Fusion, Ian attempts to make amends, and Vicki and Ross think about wedding venues.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: No flights, no phones - and one high-stakes, low-budget race to the finish. On a globe-spanning adventure of a lifetime, which pair will cross the line first?

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its week 10, and Lord Sugars candidates are unleashed on the pet industry. Battling it out for a place in the final five, both teams must design a pet product and create a social media campaign before pitching for orders from some of the UKs biggest retailers. In the boardroom, the teams are left chasing their tails, and at least one candidate goes walkies.

programme.desc: Central Leeds crew, Nagina and Mo, are called to a scene where police are negotiating an end to a siege where a man is threatening to harm himself and the officers attending.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fly-Tippers Get Caught in the Act
programme.desc: Farmers confront criminals, wildfires rage and hidden horrors emerge on a Welsh farm.

programme.title: Crimewatch Caught
programme.desc: A van driver calls 999 after making a horrific discovery and police must determine if hes telling the truth or covering a terrible crime. Plus the hunt to catch a right-wing extremist.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Special with Jacqui and Frankie from The Traitors
programme.desc: Jacqui has recruited Frankie from The Traitors, but will she be faithful to an auction property in Kent? We trawl the archives to see a Cornwall cottage Martin visited in 2013 that took quirky to a whole new level. Martin is also in Bristol where a mid-terrace is in pretty decent condition.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The red and blue teams find out whether they bought wisely when their items sell at auction in Lyme Bay. Both experts take a punt on small bonus buys, but for one the reward is big!

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Elaine and Paul in Mickleover love all things 50s - 50s cars, 50s clothes, 50s music and 50s dancing. But Elaine also loves fairies.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Pop superstar, H from Steps - aka Ian Watkins - along with his partner Tom, put property expert Denise Nurse through her paces as she attempts to find them the perfect family home in the beautiful South Wales countryside where H was born and raised. Denise presents a selection of homes fit for an international musical icon and the couple teaches her some of the local lingo, whilst they all try their hands at sheep herding and spill the tea on a visit to a Welsh tea plantation.

programme.title: The Sound of Music
programme.desc: Classic film version of the Rodgers and Hammerstein musical in which a novice nun leaves her convent to accept a position as governess to a large family. Despite their initial misgivings, she wins over the seven children and their disciplinarian father by sharing her love of music, but their happiness is threatened by the Nazi invasion of Austria. The film won five Oscars.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gethin Jones for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox meets restaurateur Karim, who asks for a miniature of his secondary school, St Pauls Way, a safe haven during his childhood when racism was rife in his east London neighbourhood. Miniaturist Abi painstakingly recreates the maths classroom of his favourite teacher, leaving a special clue that leads to an emotional surprise when the model is revealed.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Angellica Bell bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Iron Gran: Im in My 80s and Doing Triathlons
programme.desc: The UKs oldest female Ironman triathlete is immortalised in paint, but should artist Caroline Pool capture Eddies fun-loving energy or the quiet steeliness beneath the surface?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: A couple are targeted by a burglar who repeatedly sneaks back in to clean up without a trace. With several potential suspects, Humphrey must uncover who is out for revenge.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Roy Wood Jr hosts the first in a new series, with guest panellists Armando Iannucci and journalist Anushka Asthana joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the latest news.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Finally back together, Conor and Jock meet an older couple who seem similar in many ways, then accidentally take them hostage.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Elaine and Paul in Mickleover love all things 50s - 50s cars, 50s clothes, 50s music and 50s dancing. But Elaine also loves fairies.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Pop superstar, H from Steps - aka Ian Watkins - along with his partner Tom, put property expert Denise Nurse through her paces as she attempts to find them the perfect family home in the beautiful South Wales countryside where H was born and raised. Denise presents a selection of homes fit for an international musical icon and the couple teaches her some of the local lingo, whilst they all try their hands at sheep herding and spill the tea on a visit to a Welsh tea plantation.

programme.title: The Sound of Music
programme.desc: Classic film version of the Rodgers and Hammerstein musical in which a novice nun leaves her convent to accept a position as governess to a large family. Despite their initial misgivings, she wins over the seven children and their disciplinarian father by sharing her love of music, but their happiness is threatened by the Nazi invasion of Austria. The film won five Oscars.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gethin Jones for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox meets restaurateur Karim, who asks for a miniature of his secondary school, St Pauls Way, a safe haven during his childhood when racism was rife in his east London neighbourhood. programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels to Indonesia in search of three very different reptiles to add to his list of 60 deadly predators. He and his crew have a nerve-racking close encounter when they are chased by a group of ravenous, three metre long Komodo Dragons. Steve also demonstrates the incredible gliding technique of a tiny flying lizard and heads out on a nocturnal hunt for the beautiful but lethal Green Pit Viper.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are set the task of creating a TV advert for Volkswagen. Despite vowing to work together to deliver a classic piece of advertising, the boys quickly fall out on set. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond looks for an alternative to boring Germanic sports saloons and ends up going mostly sideways in the new Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst. programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Roadtrip
programme.desc: Italians are some of the happiest people in Europe, and Bologna, capital of the Emilia Romagna region, is known as one of the happiest places in Italy. While the Pasta Sisters teach Clive how to make tortellini and reveal why even Italians think Bologna is the best place to eat, Clive dons his hard hat and scales some scaffolding to find out why this city is known as the mediaeval Manhattan.

programme.desc: Coming from the centre of historic Oxford, the venue is the magnificent Sheldonian Theatre still used today for lectures and music recitals. Paul visits the drawing and print room at The Ashmolean Museum.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Amma surprise Bing to show him Sulas sparkly painted butterfly face. Bing also wants a painted face, one that looks like Biteysaurus, and Sula says she can do it for him. Sula begins smothering his face in Biteys signature green, but when she accidentally adds purple spots, Bing is horrified! He wants Sula to go home. But then Flop adds some teeth to Bings Biteysaurus, so he can really be Bitey.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Follows the adventures of a Blue Heeler puppy, Bluey, who lives with her mum, dad, and sister. Her energy and lovable spirit gets her into all kinds of funny and unpredictable situations.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Foz tries to play an old Spanish guitar, and Xuli is delighted when her first ever souvenir selfie flutters out of it. It shows her on holiday as a child in a Spanish town with her long-lost friend Maria. Foz suggests they visit Maria, but theres a problem: Xuli was so young when she used to holiday there that she cant remember where it was! Luckily, Fozs keen eyes quickly spot an important clue, and soon theyre searching across Spain in hope of a reunion.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit

programme.desc: Five and friends set off on a rhyming romp through field and forest but they keep getting mixed up.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: H makes a hen, who lays an egg, which rolls away. The `Alphablocks hop to it, give chase and discover the egg sat on a wall, about to have a great fall.

programme.title: Love Monster

programme.title: Playtime Towers

programme.title: Roots and Fruits

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Coco is showing Bing how magnets work. While Coco builds a tower, Bing wants to find more magnetic things. In the garden he tests the ball, table and key, announcing which are magnetic. When Bing tries the watering can, he accidentally drops the magnet behind the flowerbed! Neither Bing nor Coco can reach it, so Flop shows them how to fish with magnets.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `pasta, and goes on a whirlwind word adventure all about pasta: yellow, green and even rainbow pasta. Dee and a friend make some cheesy pasta, and Ant goes for a ride in a pasta balloon!

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Biba gets frustrated when Lu abandons their cushion castle to play with baby Bo.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Reg asks the Piggies to put on a show for the Bunnies. Cesar builds a stage, while Cornelia and Charlie gather performer. They decide it will be a circus show. In the end, the Piggies are clowns transforming the stage into a big top!

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a field with an obstacle course. They watch an athlete going over the hurdles and decide to try going over them themselves. As the hurdles get bigger, going over gets harder and harder.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Today, Justin and his friend get creative with paint at a pottery cafe. Artiste Tumble also has an arty day, painting a portrait of Tumble Ted.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies become a pop group and perform a pop song. In Tummy Tales, children watch a one-man band playing music and accompany him by playing their own music.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee plays I Spy whilst on a camping trip with her new friend Theodore.

programme.title: I Can Grow It

Abbie and James join Aaron in the garden for a fun game of big plant, small plant, taking it in turns to spot plants of different sizes. They soon find a tiny one thats just poked its head above the soil - but where has it come from?

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Murphy is in the middle of a mega clean-up in the attic, but he cant find Rebeccas lost hat. His magic cleaning machine goes wrong and causes mayhem, but it does give him a monster make idea - a paper banana skin! The Mini Monsters create wonderful scrap bag monsters, while Rebeccas grandpa tries to create a new ice cream flavour and a dreamy creamy sundae. The Recycling Express brings an old box with a window, which Murphy and Rebecca transform into a terrific box truck.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Stuck in a cave during heavy rain, Ginger and the Peas find fun playing indoors for once.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: The family play a game where they trek around the Blue Mountains and outsmart the cheeky fox.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Tag finds an old book hidden in a tree, it is the Mythical Creature book. The Squirrels and Duggee learn and discover all about Mythical Creatures and how some are a combination of different animals.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Five Little Ducks
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver and the Penguins help reunite Mummy Duck with her babies as they sing Five Little Ducks.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along the coast and the river to a place where they can sail their boats. On the way they fly to space on a giant anchor, find a bridge with a bit missing, have a look at an old rope and meet a sleepy dragon!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Its Mummys birthday. Henry, Sophie and Mama go on a secret shopping trip to find the perfect present. Back at home, Henry plans out a treasure map and sends Mummy on a heroic birthday adventure.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Naimas family are from Algeria. Naima is cooking a special Algerian borek for her friends to try. Borek is made from pastry, mashed potato, meat, cheese and olives. The great thing about borek is that you can add lots of different ingredients depending on what you like, and thats exactly what Naima is doing today. Naima mashes her potatoes, cuts some spring onions and sprinkles in some cinnamon before she gets the table ready for her Algerian feast.

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Chantelle visits a local school that has a nature garden, whilst Rory meets an expert on slugs. Chantelle and Rorys fact files help explain all of Lucias questions. Slugs are superheroes and they help the planet in their own special way.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks is a disability assistance dog for her owner, Charli, who has a condition that makes it painful for her to pick things up or to push and pull things. Tinks uses her superfetch skills to help Charli every day. The smallest of the Dog Squad gang, Tinks is a little Lhasa Apso with lots of big dog energy. Tinks and Charli are planting some seeds at the allotments.

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet an enormous Komodo dragon and lay an extremely smelly trail for him to find food. In the wild, Komodo dragons will eat almost anything. Next, they meet the Parsons chameleon, which has an astonishing way of catching food.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.desc: Chip sings a heartfelt song about being a potato. Though he cant do many physical things, he is proud of who he is.

The Squirrels set about testing out Duggees golden rules for party etiquette by mingling with fellow guests but get a suspiciously lukewarm response.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray Barones life seems perfect: hes a successful sportswriter living with his wife Debra and three kids, but trouble comes in paradise in the shape of his interfering mother, grumpy father and resentful brother. programme.desc: Robert administers IQ tests to Ray and Debra and then tests their reactions to false results.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Soap Opera Party

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Disappointed to learn that they cannot conceive a baby naturally, Monica and Chandler consider their options. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: Karl reminisces about an overabundance of breath mints throughout his childhood. After years of nursing a childhood leg injury, Karl visits a massage therapist with dubious credentials. Ricky, Stephen and Karl describe their earliest memories and later, Karl finds an adult magazine in his doctors waiting room and learns about alternative lifestyles.

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: After contemplating the concept of memory, Ricky and Karl perform a role play where they are the two lobes of Karls brain. Karl also pretends he manages a plumbing company that he rules with an iron fist; and in Karls diary, he explains why Martians are more advanced than Earthlings, describes what happens when a horse walks into a bar, and questions whether or not were running out of new sounds.

Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Dino Hunters

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Competing to host the best party in Grimsby are straight-talking Mel and Patrick, seasoned ravers Vanessa and Steve and lovebirds Amber and Ryan, whose matching pink outfits raise eyebrows.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In West Yorkshire, there are terrible jokes, comedy teeth, plate-licking and a conga, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Kim and Pete are looking for a spacious family-friendly home where they can keep an eye on their three boys.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Rock-star turned zoo-keeper Scott spends under £100 on Ebay for a wedding dress for his paramedic bride to be, Verity - leaving him £1, 800 to spend on life-size plastic animals to decorate the reception for his jungle themed wedding.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Sea-loving groom, Dave wants to whisk his water-phobic bride-to-be, Rosie off to a remote island wedding, but its not just any island, its windswept Lundy Island off the coast of Devon, where the bride once nearly drowned and had to be rescued.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The boss of Shoppers World witnesses a robbery and discovers a lack of security, outdated technology and poor training practises when he goes undercover on the shop floor.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The boss of Muscle Maker Grill meets a franchisee that isnt following the business model and is making executive decisions about the companys meal plan programme.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon helps out at Jacks Waterfront in St. Clair Shores, Mich., a lakeside restaurant in a popular summer spot.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay heads to Cranbury, N.J., to help two friends who bought a French Bistro they used to work in; a disgusting discovery in a kitchen cooler forces Ramsay to shut the restaurant down on the busiest night of the year.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: A family of five whose lives revolve around cooking ask Drew and Jonathan to find them a new home with their dream kitchen.

programme.title: Below Deck
programme.desc: After learning some heartbreaking information about Cat, Fraser urges Barbie to be kinder toward her, which in turn ignites a new feud between Barbie and Fraser.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: In an epic final featuring dunking, dinosaurs and disastrous directions, Jamali Maddix staggers around and Mike Wozniak staggers everyone once again. Who will win Gregs head is to be decided.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts with Rachel Riley and Susie Dent. Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes. John Cooper Clarke joins Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Its finally the big day - Mark is getting married to Sophie. When Mark and his best man Jeremy set off in the car to the church, with a very hungover Super Hans in the back, he must wrestle with his crippling doubts and decide if hell go through with the wedding after all.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark rediscovers single life after his disastrous wedding, and he and Jeremy become victims of crime.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Alice gives feline fan Luke a pawsome tattoo, Sketch rids Gavin of a saucy slogan, Glen tackles Hannahs four-letter faux pas, and firefighter Simon has a hot design.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: In an epic final featuring dunking, dinosaurs and disastrous directions, Jamali Maddix staggers around and Mike Wozniak staggers everyone once again. Who will win Gregs head is to be decided.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Rick Forman, founder and CEO of New Jersey-based Forman Mills, a discount clothing superstore, goes incognito among his own staff posing as a contestant on a television reality show.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stella and Dot CEO Jessica Herrin goes under cover to find the real gems working in her company, and learns one employees true feelings about corporate management.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carl and Brianha bring the glamour of the French Riviera to Bognor Regis - with lasers and an asthma-friendly smoke machine. Will guests Mark and Lisa, and Justin and Karen, be impressed?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: High-flyers Bob and Louise, ditzy Will and Kim, and Iranian expats Pasha and Haleh compete in Cheltenham, in a week featuring massive portions, a tale of heroism and Persian rice pudding.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: With a four-year-old and another baby on the way, Mike and Nicole need more room to grow. But their current property is holding them back.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Another brave bride leaves the most important day of her life in the hands of the man she loves. Harry Potter fan Sean needs all the help he can get planning a spooky witches and wizards-themed wedding in a forest for his demanding bride-to-be Nicole. But with a fiancee set on a church for her big day and a best man whod rather watch football than plan a wedding, will Sean be able to work his magic to pull off a spellbinding day to remember or will it all be just an illusion?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Self-confessed tattoo addict Adam is on a mission to give his bride-to-be Dani an unconventional wedding at a London tattoo convention in front of 10,000 inked-up strangers, but all she wants is an intimate day with friends and family.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: The boss of Yesco goes undercover and discovers disgruntled employees graffiti and finds out how the companys quick expansion has caused some major problems.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Scott Gerber, CEO of The Gerber Group, must make cocktails and serve drinks to a highly-demanding group of customers when he goes undercover on the shop floor.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Manhattans Black Pearl, a struggling seafood restaurant with three different owners running the kitchen, has come to chef Gordon Ramsay for help; one of the owners is resistant to change and questions every move Gordon makes.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The owners of J. Willys Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, are $1.2 million in debt and contact chef Gordon Ramsay in hope that he can help give them a fresh start; Gordon teaches the inexperienced cooks how to make various dishes.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: Dennis and Marie thought they had found their dream home. Three kids later, they are feeling the squeeze and looking for a larger property.

programme.title: Below Deck
programme.desc: Barbie continues to test boundaries with Fraser; Cat struggles to find her place within the crew; Jareds personal challenges take a toll on him as Captain Kerry takes notice; Sunny deals with the consequences of mixing work and play.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The Taskmaster drags Ardal OHanlon, Bridget Christie, Chris Ramsey, Judi Love and Sophie Duker into his lair so that he can pit them against each other and watch excitedly as they lose all dignity while fighting to win his golden-head trophy.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley compete against Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Mark and Sophie return to work after their non-existent honeymoon and Mark tries to deflect the inevitable bad PR attached to a jilter. Meanwhile, Jeremy and Super Hans new band play their first gig, but it ends badly when the third member of the group locks himself in the toilet.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy finally runs out of money and finds himself on a downward spiral of hunger, homelessness and criminality. Meanwhile, Mark needs to find a girlfriend before his birthday party.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Sketch tackles Nicks measuring stick and covers Frans naughty nickname. Glen helps heathen Bob get an honour inking, and Jay removes Lees charming chat-up line.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The Taskmaster drags Ardal OHanlon, Bridget Christie, Chris Ramsey, Judi Love and Sophie Duker into his lair so that he can pit them against each other and watch excitedly as they lose all dignity while fighting to win his golden-head trophy.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Murray dont want to go on a camping trip, so father and son create a film faking it. Erica joins an all-girl a cappella group and learns an important lesson after taking over the group.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly buys a bicycle for Adam just like the one from `Pee Wees Big Adventure. Barry and Erica turn Murrays furniture store into an after-hours lounge for college students.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Aussie-born Adam is taking a risk planning an 80s Aussie soap wedding when bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil is determined to finally scare Claire on Halloween. Meanwhile, for the first time, Gloria is feeling self-conscious about her age when someone correctly assumes she is Jays wife.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Gloria is using her alone time with the twins as an opportunity to secretly baptize them before Haley and Dylan get home.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, to try to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over the failing Spin-a-Yarn restaurant in 1995 and began to turn it into a success. He fell in love with a waitress, but the restaurant and the marriage are now failing.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica, Barry, Geoff and the gang go on a spring break trip and things dont go as expected. Adam questions if college is for him when his test scores are not as good as he was hoping for.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Formica King
programme.desc: Business rival and nemesis `Formica Mike attempts to make an offer Murray cant refuse. Adam offers Ms Cinoman his and Ericas original show when William Penn cannot secure the rights to a well-known spring musical.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A surprise birthday trip to Hawaii for Jay is marred by the fact that his whole family is tagging along. The chaos begins at the airport with forgotten identification, security breaches and flying phobias.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jays vacation plans are interrupted by an unwelcome reality check and Phil tries to make the trip romantic for Claire. Meanwhile, Mitchell and Cameron have a difference of opinion.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The two new couples go honeymooning, featuring an unlikely third wheel, frank confessions, and a groom with views that infuriate his new wife.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Rude Tubes naughty little sister presents a man pretending to be a walrus, an incredible downhill bike run and some computer keyboard hurling.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Those looking for a love match this time include a self-proclaimed man-eater, a urologist whose job can scare off men, and a single mum seeking a guy who can accept that she has a child.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Italian bistro Charlies in La Verne, Southern California, which was bought in 2010 by Tatiana Leiva, her mother and her sister. Tatiana didnt actually put any money into the purchase herself, but her hard-working mother and sister did because they felt they were helping to fulfil Tatianas dream. They even put up their houses as collateral! Unfortunately, soon Charlies was in trouble.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Fat Tony is finally caught and forced to serve time, on jury duty he passes power to Dan. Dan comes to Homer for help when he realises hell have to `trim the excess fat from Tonys personnel.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa tries to cheer Homer up by winning him a `MyPad at the school auction. He soon falls back into a funk when he breaks the magical tablet device, but Ned Flanders discovers a miracle tree in the Simpsons backyard.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: It may be his birthday but poor Manny isnt in the mood to celebrate. Meanwhile, Phil and Claire engage in a car race, while Mitch and Cam get sidetracked.

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The two new couples go honeymooning, featuring an unlikely third wheel, frank confessions, and a groom with views that infuriate his new wife.

programme.title: Voyage to the Bottom of the Sea
programme.desc: A belt of radiation goes out of control and threatens to melt the Earth, leaving Admiral Nelson and his prototype nuclear submarine to salvage the situation.

programme.title: Men In Black 3 Interview Special
programme.desc: Will Smith, Josh Brolin and director Barry Sonnenfeld talk about the third of their sci-fi comedy adventures.

programme.title: Turner and Hooch
programme.desc: Turner is a meticulously groomed, excruciatingly well-organised detective working in a small California coastal town. When local character John McIntyre is murdered by drug smugglers, the only witness is McIntyres slobbery, smelly mutt Hooch. Turner, who despises dogs in general and Hooch in particular, is compelled to put the cantankerous dog up as his house guest.

programme.title: Oliver!
programme.desc: Endearing film version of Lionel Barts successful stage musical based on Dickenss tale of the fluctuating fortunes of an orphan boy. Ron Moody stands out as the wily old scrounger Fagin in a series of well-crafted set-pieces and atmospheric musical numbers, and a tribe of child stars brings the 19th-century London underworld vividly to life.

programme.title: Men in Black International Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.

programme.title: Hidden Figures
programme.desc: NASA mathematicians Katherine Johnson, Dorothy Vaughan and Mary Jackson cross gender and race lines to help launch astronaut John Glenn into outer space.

After crashing on an unknown planet, two survivors quickly discover theyre actually stranded on Earth, 65 million years ago. With only one chance at a rescue, they must make their way across an unknown terrain riddled with prehistoric creatures.

programme.title: The Predator
programme.desc: From the outer reaches of space to the small-town streets of suburbia, the hunt comes home. The universes most lethal hunters are stronger, smarter and deadlier than ever before, having genetically upgraded themselves with DNA from other species. When a boy accidentally triggers their return to Earth, only a ragtag crew of ex-soldiers and an evolutionary biologist can prevent the end of the human race.

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths
programme.desc: A struggling screenwriter finds himself inadvertently entangled in the criminal underworld when his knucklehead friends abduct a mobsters beloved Shih Tzu.

The universes most lethal hunters are stronger, smarter and deadlier than ever before, having genetically upgraded themselves with DNA from other species. When a boy accidentally triggers their return to Earth, only a ragtag crew of ex-soldiers and an evolutionary biologist can prevent the end of the human race.

programme.title: Seven Psychopaths
programme.desc: A struggling screenwriter finds himself inadvertently entangled in the criminal underworld when his knucklehead friends abduct a mobsters beloved Shih Tzu.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge together, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop. Theres a fishing trip over to Rathlin Island on the menu too, where James tastes some dishes made from the local kelp. James and Paul then visit the Old Lammas Fair, where they taste some of the delicious seafood.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers are back in New Orleans, where Si tries to convince Dave to enjoy jazz. They also cook some classic local dishes such as jambalaya and gumbo.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her secrets of home cooking and celebrating.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her secrets of home cooking and celebrating.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes lasagne with roasted duck, the ultimate bacon sarnie and, from rural Mexico, huevos rancheros - beautifully cooked eggs with a spicy tomato salsa, black beans, avocado and a soft tortilla.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie makes fish and chips with an Indian pale ale batter. Theres a treat for vegetarians with Jamies version of the Indonesian salad gado gado and lastly theres a speedy crab linguine.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina takes inspiration from the fabulous food found in the Hamptons to create new recipes in a piece for her Barefoot blog. She makes salmon and guacamole sandwiches and salted caramel brownies.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: A smoked salmon and bagel masterclass, a lesson in steak, coffee roastery in Queens, spice rubbed New York strip steak, sweet potato fries, coffee granita.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: For alternative ingredients week, the bakers must bake without sugar, gluten or dairy. The signature challenge requires sugar free cakes, the technical challenge is gluten free pittas and the showstopper calls for dairy free ice cream rolls.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its pastry week and the bakers begin with frangipane tarts for the signature challenge. The technical challenge calls for the flaouna, a traditional cheese filled pastry from Cyprus. The showstopper is the classic 70s canape, vol au vents.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James adventure brings him over the border into North Wales, where hes joined by chef Stephen Terry for this leg of his journey. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking up a real treat - Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb. James offers his own variation of Welsh lamb in the grounds of the hall, serving with gnocchi and vegetables. Next, one of James boyhood dreams comes true as he has the ultimate falconry experience.

programme.title: James Martins Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Stephen head south through Wales en route to Pembrokeshire. But before they get there, they stop to visit Gareth Ward and his stunning restaurant Ynyshir. After filling their bellies, they make it to the coast where James cooks up his Marloes Sands seafood parcel. Stephen tracks down Wales answer to Willy Wonka, young chocolatier Liam Burgess, who helps him make a very special one-off-chocolate bar for James.

Theres a fishing trip over to Rathlin Island on the menu too, where James tastes some dishes made from the local kelp. programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to Northern Ireland with his old chef buddy Paul Rankin, where theyre exploring the area around Lough Neagh, the biggest freshwater lake in the British Isles. James and Paul discover a wasabi farm, a rarity outside of Japan. Next, Paul visits an old friend, a local honey producer, while James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion: the highest point in Armagh.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: All San Diego, All the Time
programme.desc: In Ocean Beach, Calif., a surfer spot with righteous burgers, a noodle joint with fried rice, pho, and a story to tell, in North Park, Calif., pizza.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Carlsbad, Calif., ravioli, in Troutdale, Ore., sloppy joes, in San Francisco, quesadillas, tortas and pozole rojo.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In San Francisco, a totally gluten-free menu of Venezuelan standbys like arepas and empanadas, in Portland, homemade meats like Italian red wine dry cured salami and roasted garlic and rosemary bologna.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys finding authentic eats in unusual spots. In St Louis, theres a New Orleans roadhouse serving up rice and beans, cajun pot-stickers and pulled pork pasta.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey travels to historic Boston for some epic New England eats, including a burger topped with Thanksgiving favourites, a plate-sized serving of prime rib and a 4.5-pound Irish breakfast challenge at Flann OBriens Pub.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to Burlington, Vermont, where he devours a beer-infused bagel feast and a burger with donut buns made in a school bus. Casey participates in the Chuck Norris challenge at Handys Lunch.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy has to convince his mother that after 100 years it is time to give Carlos a timely facelift, so he cooks up a plan to sweeten the deal.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Angela, the owner of a Brazilian restaurant, pays a visit to Carlos to find out if Buddys team can bake a carnival cake that can match her vibrant parties.

programme.title: Michel Rouxs Provence Masterclass
programme.desc: Michel takes Sunday lunch to the next level. He serves fish quenelles, chicken cooked in salt crust pastry and red wine poached pears with galette serpentine.

programme.title: Michel Rouxs Provence Masterclass
programme.desc: Michel serves up a feast thats made to share. He prepares a smoked fish platter, a rich beef cheek bourguignon and a lattice-topped salted caramel and walnut tart.

programme.title: Michel Rouxs French Country Cooking
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr showcases the unique cuisine of Southern France. He shares his secrets to easily mastering French country cooking at home.

programme.title: Michel Rouxs French Country Cooking
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr showcases the unique cuisine of Southern France. He shares his secrets to easily mastering French country cooking at home.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: Michel goes fishing with an old friend and makes an alfresco fish dish by the river. Plus, he makes watercress soup, a winter salad, and roast apples.

programme.title: Roux Down The River
programme.desc: In Southend, Michel meets a family of fishmongers and chefs keeping it local. Plus, he makes scallop curry, prawn toast, and fried custard beignets.

programme.title: Roux Down the River
programme.desc: Michel Roux finds the perfect fish and chips and visits Monkey Island at Royal Windsor. He cooks a mackerel burger, shepherds pie, and a peach meringue.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge Secrets of the Pub Kitchen
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge picks popular items on the menus at modern British pubs and gives them his own twist. He takes inspiration from modern gastropubs to create simple, contemporary, and delicious recipes.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge Secrets of the Pub Kitchen
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge picks popular items on the menus at modern British pubs and gives them his own twist. He takes inspiration from modern gastropubs to create simple, contemporary, and delicious recipes.

programme.title: Tom Kerridges Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom brings an all-time favourite dish to his Sunday table: fish and chips. He bakes a beautiful side of salmon in brown butter and makes perfect triple-cooked chips.

programme.title: Tom Kerridges Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom pays homage to the South of France. He makes a seafood bouillabaisse with sea bass, red mullet and shellfish, and praline and chocolate profiteroles.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary cooks up a celebration of five delicious dishes suitable for those special days, when youre having people around for dinner or simply want to treat a loved one at home. To start, Mary prepares some delicious salmon fillets with herbs and red pepper with a foolproof side of spiralized veg, perfect for any dinner party.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary cooks up a celebration of six delicious dishes perfect for outdoor entertaining and lazy summer days. To start, she prepares a luxurious whole sea bass with lime and dill butter that couldnt be simpler.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Easter Feast
programme.desc: Mary discovers the traditions followed by diverse British families and communities during Lent and Good Friday and the food that brings them together as each prepare for the great Easter celebration.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Easter Feast
programme.desc: Mary explores the wonderful foods that bring each different community together on Easter Sunday, the most symbolic and meaningful feast in the Christian calendar. As well as sharing her own family favourites, like mouth-watering Roast Lamb, Mary discovers how the Greek Orthodox community break the fast with Tsoureki bread; spends a day with the Archbishop of York, John Sentamu, cooking up a storm in his kitchen; and discovers Filipino and Italian Easter specialities.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Its all go as Rick celebrates the abundance of seafood and the rich colourful sauces of Naples, or Napoli, and visits Suffolk to experience North Sea cod with cabbage and beer.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Its home and away for seafood fan Rick as he catches turbot off the Cornish coast and cooks it with cider and cream, and travels to Goa for prawn caldene and spicy Goan lobster.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Seafood king Ricks off to Galicia in Spain to sample some rare shellfish. Plus recipes for hake with peppers and saffron, and clams in a marinara sauce.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: This programme looks at the preparation of a spicy shark vindaloo and the making of an authentic dhal with lentils. Plus a visit to Chesapeake Bay in America, where freshwater oysters and crab cakes are the order of the day.

programme.title: Jamies Fish Suppers
programme.desc: Chef Jamie Oliver presents recipes to inspire fish lovers to try different types of seafood.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: On the menu are flashy fish stew, saffron sauce and garlic bread, plus seared Asian beef, best noodle salad and ginger dressing.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes his best fish baps, mushy peas and tartare sauce. Later, he cooks mushroom farfalle with blue cheese and a hazelnut and apple salad.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: White Fish Tagine and Spicy Cajun Chicken
programme.desc: Jamie cooks white fish tagine, carrot, mint and clementine salad, plus spicy Cajun chicken, smashed sweet potato and fresh corn salsa.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Asian Fish and Ultimate Pork Tacos
programme.desc: Asian fish, miso noodles and crunchy veg, and ultimate pork tacos, spicy black beans and avocado garden salad.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks grilled tuna, `kinda Niçoise salad, Thai chicken laksa and mildly-spiced noodle squash broth.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up seared Asian tuna, coconut rice and jiggy jiggy greens, plus ricotta fritters, tomato sauce and courgette salad.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Green Tea Salmon and Modern Greek Salad
programme.desc: Featuring green tea salmon, coconut rice and miso greens, plus modern Greek salad, spinach, chickpea and feta parcels.

programme.title: Jamies 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: On the menu is smoked salmon, Yorkshire pudding, beets and asparagus, plus gorgeous Greek chicken, sweet pepper and pea couscous with tzatziki.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey travels to Louisville, Kentucky, for some epic eats, including a booze-infused Southern staple, a one-of-a-kind spicy popcorn, and frozen hot chocolate challenge in nearby Santa Claus.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey visits St, Louis, Missouri, in search of the biggest and best the city has to offer. He encounters a four-pound meatball and tackles a New York-style pizza so massive hell need the help of a partner to take it down.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Barbecue wings and ribs in Brooklyn, NY; crab quesadillas, tuna tacos and shrimp in North Carolina.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys warming up with some stand-out specialties. In Brooklyn is a mom and pop diner flipping an apple waffle with pancetta, and deep-frying breakfast empanadas.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emilys David Bowie inspired dish celebrates Britains food heroes and shes using Cornish duck meat from her local producer that she pairs with grilled radicchio and Malbec sauce. Joe has sourced his ingredients locally too, using hay from his family farm and Jersey beef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs need to impress the esteemed judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the pop stars the banquet will celebrate. They are joined by one half of the synthpop duo The Communards, Reverend Richard Coles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom `Westy Westerland is a former National Chef of Wales and head chef of the Brasserie at Lucknam Park in Wiltshire. Cindy Challoner is the former head chef at prestigious teaching restaurant `The Classroom in Cardiff. They face Andy Sheriden formerly of Sosban restaurant in Llanelli, returning to the competition for his second attempt at making it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Andy cooks venison in celebration of his favourite band, Oasis. He accompanies the loin with rhubarb and beetroot ketchup, black pudding puree, pickled beetroot and a pistachio crumb. Westys beef dish `The Cow Shed is named after a former stage at the Glastonbury festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from Wales must cook their four course menus again. They must take on board their veterans advice as they serve their tributes to Britains music success.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune

programme.desc: The chefs face their toughest critic yet when tasked with elevating a classic Southern staple, but communication breakdowns then threaten to derail dinner service and leave Chef Ramsay fuming - from uneven cooking temperatures to poor timing between stations, even the simplest mistakes could spell the end for one contestant.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liam leaves for work as a distraught Maria packs her bag for the hospital, unable to find a way of telling him about the baby. Meanwhile, Jack learns Paul has been charged.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maria continues to keep Liam in the dark as she heads back to the Street. Bill lets slip to a perplexed Gail about Audrey meeting Ted, and Liz refuses to go on Vernons cruise.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: There is plenty on Frosts plate when senior citizens are targeted in a string of vicious attacks, but at least he can call on his attractive new female sergeant for help.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordlys winter pheasant shoot is disrupted when the entire contents of his game larder are stolen. Nick investigates an apparent crime wave in Aidensfield after a mysterious spate of burglaries, his enquiries make him believe that an incestuous relationship is the reason behind the thefts.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The annual funfair comes to Aidensfield, causing much excitement but some ill feeling too. When the proprietor of a nearby caravan site complains about the noise and mess, Nick tells her there is nothing he can do. Then a five-year-old boy goes missing.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott delve into the murder of a visitor to Plummers Relish factory, and discover that the dead man was employed by a rival company. Further investigation reveals a web of secrets and lies within the Plummer family, and a campaign to frighten the family matriarch - but why?

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Claire discovers the extent of the familys financial difficulties when Ashley admits to using their own money to prop up the business. Audrey makes a disturbing discovery.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audreys shocking discovery prompts Maria to confess her distressing secret, Marcus is suspicious when Sean returns early from holiday and Chesney is upset by Less news.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A break-in at the surgery in Aidensfield heralds the beginning of a serious investigation for Nick. Pills that were stolen in the raid are being passed off as other drugs and sold around the area. Kate discovers the cause of one of her patients previous miscarriages.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is warned that Steve Walsh, an ex-convict, may be heading his way. In the night Aidensfield gets cut off as a severe snow storm presents itself and the whole area is unreachable. Meanwhile, Kate is called out by a man, whose partner is in labour and about to give birth.

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Crime drama. A high-stakes criminal trial of a ruthless Brighton drug lord and a puzzling double murder investigation push Graces team to the very brink.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a mans naked body is found at the centre of a crop circle and more corpses begin turning up with peculiar injuries, DCI Barnaby has to uncover who is responsible - or are extraterrestrial powers really at work in Midsomer?

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the death of Professor Miranda Thornton, who apparently killed herself after her dating video was leaked on to a vicious media blog. However, Lewis does not believe that Thorntons death was suicide and he has qualms about the blogs founder, Kit Renton, and the owner of the dating website, Sophie Leland. When one of Rentons employees is found murdered, the police realise she must have been killed.

programme.desc: Arthur decides to buy a toupee to impress a clippie.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi finds herself in the middle of an unusual mystery, when she sets about restoring a painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost. Brought into the Repair Shop by Andrew Winter, the portrait depicts his mother as a young woman, and having been left languishing in an attic for decades, it has become torn and discoloured over time.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: With a swirl of tartan and a burst of the bagpipes, our Scottish tour continues in Fife with antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore. The 1960s Rolls Royce glides the pair through countryside and town as they head from the east coast to central Scotland. Natashas money reserves are dwindling, so she cautiously settles on a bargain piece of Scottish studio pottery. However, a canny buy of an arts and crafts-style lantern pays dividends at the auction in Hamilton.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On October 2, 1987, a Boston police officer responding to a domestic disturbance call was fatally wounded. Local authorities launched a citywide manhunt, but were only able to find the fingerprints of the chief suspect.

programme.desc: A hairstylist was murdered by her ex-boyfriend, and a woman discovered that her husband was lying about everything.

programme.sub-title: Dark Clouds Lead to a Storm
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman turns up savagely murdered in a bar parking lot; as investigators retrace her last known steps, several suspects with possible motives come on their radar.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In Tulsa, Okla., Amanda Douglas and Jennifer Sudar Sanders enjoy an evening out at a casino; the next morning, the two are shot outside their apartment complex; investigators soon learn that the shooter was driving a distinctive gold Pontiac.

programme.desc: What exposed Tracie Andrews lie that a road rage attack killed her lover? Mike Gifford-Hulls TV plea for his missing wife Farsi hides the truth.

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death
